creator: jwagner@graffadmin.com
text:
I have been troubleshooting and more information on this, confusing 
though it may be.

I was able to find version 2.6.2_3,1 of the amavisd-new port online and 
used this to compile and install amavisd-new after deinstalling 2.6.4.  
The child processes no longer die when newsyslog is run.  However, the 
amavisd-new child processes were still dying quite often.

Digging in some more, I discovered that whenever I ran postqueue -f to 
flush the queued mail, the amavisd child processes would die several 
times.  There were 6 messages 'stuck' in the mail queue, the system 
saying 'lost connection with 127.0.0.1[127.0.0.1] while sending end of 
data -- message may be sent more than once' for the status reported by 
postqueue -p.  I would guess that this was happening when the message 
was being passed to amavisd-new for virus scanning.  (The anti-spam 
controls are disabled in amavisd-new.)

Using webmin, I was able to read these messages and manually copied the 
contents into a new e-mail to their intended recipients.  (I copied 
/var/spool/postfix/deferred so that I have a raw copy of these 
messages.)  After deleting these 6 messages, the down-graded amavisd-new 
and latest postfix 2.6.5,1 both seem to be behaving themselves.

It seems unlikely, but is it possible that amavisd-new is choking only 
on messages with a certain structure?  Nothing appeared to be special 
about the messages that were stuck in the queue to my eye, but I'm by no 
means an expert when it comes to e-mail at this level.  I'm willing to 
e-mail a tarball of the deferred directory with the stuck messages to 
somebody for debugging if it's treated as confidential.

I will continue to monitor and if another message or set of messages 
gets stuck in the same way or if the amavisd-new child processes start 
dying again, will report back.
